notes: Lower score is better under the contest cost function.
Large preplaced cases use obstacle-aware interior shelf packing so movable units can occupy legal gaps around preplaced rectangles instead of being forced to the right of them.
The obstacle-aware path is enabled only for 116-block and larger cases with preplaced rectangles, targeting the score-dominant range while preserving hard feasibility.
A selective second interior shift pass on 117- and 119-block cases reduces high-count HPWL while preserving hard feasibility.
Fixed-shape, non-preplaced interior blocks may participate in the guarded shift pass on 117- through 119-block cases, preserving dimensions while reducing high-count HPWL.

The 116- through 119-block shift pass also tests a guarded combined-axis move after independent axis clamps, accepting only overlap-free incident-wirelength improvements inside the existing bounding box.
A trimmed 120-block interior shift pass targets only the highest-connectivity free blocks to reduce incident wirelength without losing the runtime cap.
A guarded 120-block top-edge boundary compaction lowers the dominant weighted case area and HPWL without increasing soft violations.
The 116- through 119-block row-width settings were retuned from validation sweeps to reduce high-count HPWL and area while preserving 100/100 feasibility.
The 120-block row-width setting was retuned to lower the dominant weighted case HPWL and bounding-box area while preserving soft violations and 100/100 feasibility.

Boundary ordering uses incident-edge key caches only on 116-block and larger cases, reducing score-dominant runtime without lowering the full-run median runtime enough to hurt relative runtime scoring.
A narrow equal-shape swap pass is enabled only on 117- and 119-block cases, improving HPWL where the proxy stays under the runtime cap.
The 120-block free-block shift pass checks the top 20 connectivity-weighted candidates for two passes, improving the dominant weighted case HPWL while staying below the runtime cap.
The free-block shift pass pre-resolves valid pin coordinates into its adjacency cache, preserving layouts while reducing tensor-index overhead in high-count local wirelength checks.
A bounded 120-block equal-shape swap probe accepts up to two meaningful HPWL-improving swaps while preserving soft violations and bounding-box area.
